---
title: "Sports Marketing Analytics"
subtitle: "Data-Driven Fan Engagement Strategies"
author: "Ethan Lew"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    center: true
    navigation-mode: linear
    controls: true
    progress: true
    footer: "Ethan Lew | Sports Marketing Portfolio"
    code-overflow: wrap
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---
---

# Overview & Objectives

## What This Covers

- Fan engagement trends  
- Platform performance  
- Content strategy insights  
- Data-driven recommendations  


# Main Objectives

::: columns
::: column
- Analyze fan engagement trends  
- Compare platform performance  
:::

::: column
- Identify successful strategies  
- Provide marketing recommendations  
:::
:::
## Topics Covered

- Sports marketing analytics
- Fan engagement trends
- Social media performance
- Data-driven marketing strategies
- Campaign recommendations

# Why Sports Marketing Analytics Matters

## The Growing Role of Data

Modern sports organizations rely heavily on analytics to understand fan behavior and improve digital marketing performance.

## Importance

- Enhances fan engagement strategies  
- Enables targeted audience segmentation  
- Measures campaign effectiveness  
- Supports sponsorship and branding decisions  
- Drives ticket sales and digital interaction 

# Research Objectives

## Goals of the Analysis

- Identify high-performing content types
- Compare engagement across platforms
- Analyze audience interaction trends
- Recommend marketing improvements

# Social Media Engagement Trends

## Key Findings

- Short-form video content generated the highest interaction rates
- Engagement increased significantly during playoff periods
- Interactive posts performed better than static graphics
- Younger audiences were most active on Instagram and TikTok

# Engagement Metrics

| Platform | Engagement Rate | Key Insight |
|---|---:|---|
| TikTok | 11.2% | Highest audience interaction |
| Instagram | 9.8% | Strong visual engagement |
| X / Twitter | 5.4% | Effective for live updates |
| Facebook | 4.1% | Lower engagement among younger users |

# Example Visualization

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(ggplot2)

platforms <- c("Instagram", "TikTok", "X", "Facebook")
engagement <- c(9.8, 11.2, 5.4, 4.1)

df <- data.frame(platforms, engagement)

ggplot(df, aes(x = platforms, y = engagement)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Social Media Engagement Rates",
    x = "Platform",
    y = "Engagement Rate (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18)
```

# Marketing Recommendations

## Focus on What Drives Engagement

- Prioritize **short-form video content**
- Align campaigns with **high-traffic events (e.g., playoffs)**
- Increase **interactive content (polls, Q&A, stories)**
- Double down on **TikTok + Instagram growth**

---

# Skills Demonstrated

## Analytics + Communication

::: columns
::: column
### Technical

- Data visualization  
- Marketing analytics  
- Statistical analysis  
- Dashboard design  
:::

::: column
### Tools

- R / ggplot2  
- Tableau  
- Google Analytics  
- Quarto  
:::
:::

---

# Campaign Impact

## Measurable Improvements

- ↑ Fan engagement rates  
- ↑ Visibility during key campaigns  
- ↑ Platform-specific targeting effectiveness  
- ↑ Insight into fan behavior  

---

# Key Takeaways

## What This Analysis Shows

- Data is **central to modern sports marketing**
- Engagement depends on **timing + content format**
- **Short-form + interactive** content performs best
- Clear visuals improve **decision-making**

---

# Future Focus

## Next Steps for Growth

- Fan sentiment analysis  
- Predictive marketing models  
- Advanced sports analytics  
- Interactive dashboards  

---

# Career Interests

## Where I’m Headed

- Sports Marketing  
- Marketing Analytics  
- Social Media Strategy  
- Brand Management  
- Fan Engagement Analysis  

---

# Thank You

## Questions?

Ethan Lew  
Cal Poly Pomona  
Sports Marketing • Analytics • Fan Engagement
